name: Fisherman's Cottage
shortDescription: Simple wooden home for working class citizens near the water.
fullDescription: A modest wooden structure housing Venice's vital fishing families. These humble cottages typically include a ground floor for nets and equipment with living quarters above. The proximity to water is essential for their livelihood.
flavorText: The fishermen rise before the bells of San Marco, for the lagoon's bounty favors the early and the bold.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Cannot be built in San Marco or Rialto
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Must be within 3 tiles of water

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian fisherman's cottage under construction, simple wooden frame structure being built, partial timber walls, thatched roof partially completed, small construction site with basic tools, wooden ladder, timber piles nearby, modest size, located near water edge, small dock being constructed, workers installing window frames, historically accurate but simply designed, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, weathered wood textures

specialAbilities: Provides modest housing for working class
Can serve as small fishing operation base

seasonalEffects.winter: Smoke from chimney more visible, frost on roof

name: Artisan's House
shortDescription: Modest residence with attached workshop space for craftspeople.
fullDescription: A practical combined living and working space for Venice's skilled artisans. The ground floor typically houses a workshop open to the street, while upper floors provide living quarters for the artisan's family. These buildings form the backbone of Venice's craft economy.
flavorText: The hands shape the clay, the wood, the glass—and in turn, they shape Venice itself.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Cannot be built in San Marco
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Must be adjacent to street or small plaza
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Small increase in property value for nearby Basic Residences

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian artisan's house under construction, two-story structure, brick and timber frame, ground floor workshop area taking shape with large front opening, separate residential upper floor, wooden scaffolding, workers installing roof tiles, window frames being fitted, tools and construction materials organized neatly, period-appropriate construction techniques, partial plaster covering on some walls, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, warm brick and timber textures
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian artisan's house, two-story structure with workshop on ground floor, large front opening with wooden shutters that open to street, residential upper floor with small balcony, plastered exterior in warm Venetian red with exposed timber framing, terracotta roof tiles, workshop signage hanging from decorative iron bracket, small side courtyard, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, detailed craft-specific elements visible in workshop, well-worn entrance threshold, chimney with thin smoke

specialAbilities: Provides space for small craft business

seasonalEffects.carnival: Workshop busy with mask or costume production

name: Merchant's House
unlockCondition: 5,000 ducats total wealth, Merchant Guild connection
shortDescription: Comfortable residence with dedicated office space for business dealings.
fullDescription: A respectable home for Venice's merchant class, featuring separate areas for business and family life. Typically includes a ground floor counting room or office, a piano nobile (noble floor) for family living, and often additional floors for storage or servants. Represents success in Venice's trading world.
flavorText: Ledgers below, living above—the merchant's house is a perfect balance of commerce and comfort, just as the merchant balances risk and reward.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Best suited to Cannaregio, Castello, or Dorsoduro
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Preferably near commercial area or canal
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Increases property value of adjacent buildings by 5%

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian merchant's house under construction, three-story structure, substantial stone foundation visible, brick walls partially constructed, wooden scaffolding surrounding building, dignified facade taking shape with arched entryway, workers installing decorative stone window frames, ground floor office area with secure features being built, separate entrance for residential portion, construction materials of higher quality than basic residences, period-appropriate construction techniques, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, sophisticated architectural elements emerging
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian merchant's house, elegant three-story structure, dignified plastered facade in warm ochre with stone trim, Gothic Venetian arched windows on piano nobile (second floor), ground floor with secure office featuring decorative iron window guards, separate entrance for business and family, small private courtyard at rear, terracotta roof with small rooftop terrace, decorative chimney pots, quality wooden shutters, small ornamental balcony on main floor, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, subtle architectural flourishes showing prosperity without ostentation, business sign mounted beside office entrance

soundDesign.ambientSound: Quill scratching on paper, quiet business discussions
soundDesign.activitySounds: Coins counting, document shuffling, door knocking

seasonalEffects.winter: Heated interior visible through windows, elegant winter decorations
seasonalEffects.carnival: Quality decorations, guests in masks entering and leaving

name: Canal House
unlockCondition: 10,000 ducats total wealth, Water Transport Guild connection
shortDescription: Multi-story residence with direct canal access for convenient transportation.
fullDescription: A practical yet prestigious home with a private water entrance (porta d'acqua) directly on a canal. These versatile buildings allow residents to receive goods and visitors directly by boat. The water-level entrance typically includes a small private dock and storage area, with living quarters above.
flavorText: In Venice, a door to the water is worth more than a door to the street—for water is the true street of the Serenissima.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Must be built directly on a canal
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Requires direct canal frontage, private mooring
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Water quality affects property value, increases value of nearby buildings by 8%

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian canal house under construction, three-story structure being built directly on canal edge, foundation reinforcement visible at water level, water entrance taking shape with dock area, stone arched water gate under construction, brick walls rising on upper floors, wooden scaffolding with workers, ornate window frames being installed, boat delivering construction materials, temporary wooden walkways over water for construction access, isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, combination of stone and brick textures, typical Venetian construction techniques
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian canal house, elegant three-story structure rising directly from canal water, ornate water entrance with arched stone porta d'acqua (water door), small private dock with mooring poles (pali da ormeggio) painted with spiral stripes, gondola moored at entrance, second and third floors feature Gothic Venetian windows, warm terracotta plastered exterior with exposed stone trim, decorative water-level stonework showing water marks, flower boxes on upper windows, small rooftop altana (wooden roof terrace), street-side entrance with stone steps, historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, weathered waterline details, ornamental iron tie-rods visible on facade

soundDesign.ambientSound: Water lapping against foundation, boat traffic
soundDesign.activitySounds: Boat docking, goods being unloaded, water gate opening

seasonalEffects.winter: Fog rising from canal, ice protection at waterline
seasonalEffects.acqua alta: Water barriers at doorways, elevated walkways visible
seasonalEffects.carnival: Colorful lanterns hanging over water entrance, decorated gondola

name: Patrician Palazzo
unlockCondition: 50,000 ducats total wealth, Nobility status or connection
shortDescription: Grand family residence for Venice's patrician class, featuring impressive architecture and private courtyard.
fullDescription: A statement of wealth and status for Venice's noble families, the palazzo combines imposing architecture with practical business needs. Organized around a central courtyard, these buildings typically feature grand reception rooms, a family chapel, extensive servant quarters, and business offices. The facade displays the family's status through architectural refinement rather than ostentatious display.
flavorText: Stone and legacy intertwined—a patrician palazzo houses not merely a family, but a dynasty's ambitions across generations.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Preferred in San Marco, Cannaregio, or Castello
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Requires large plot, preferably with canal access
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Significantly increases property value of surrounding buildings by 15%

name: Grand Canal Palace
unlockCondition: 200,000 ducats total wealth, Great Council membership
shortDescription: Spectacular palace facing the Grand Canal, the ultimate statement of wealth and power in Venice.
fullDescription: The pinnacle of Venetian residential architecture, these magnificent palaces line the Grand Canal, their facades functioning as public displays of family power. Featuring enormous reception halls, multiple courtyards, private docks, and lavish decoration, these buildings combine Gothic, Byzantine, and Renaissance elements in a uniquely Venetian style. Only the wealthiest and most powerful families maintain such residences.
flavorText: The facade speaks to the canal, the canal speaks to the world—and both pronounce the glory of your house for generations to come.

locationRequirements.districtRestrictions: Must be built directly on the Grand Canal
locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Requires Grand Canal frontage, large plot
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Dramatically increases property value of all buildings within vicinity by 25%
